title: Metal working production and maintenance fitters and technicians
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 119 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information

Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Instruct other pilots and student pilots in aircraft operations and teach company regulations and procedures.
Coordinate flight activities with ground crews and air traffic control, and inform crew members of flight and test procedures.
Use instrumentation to guide flights and pilot aircraft when visibility is poor.
Respond to and report in-flight emergencies and malfunctions, and conduct in-flight tests and evaluations.
Choose routes, altitudes, and speeds providing the fastest, safest, and smoothest flights and plan flights according to regulations.
File instrument flight plans with air traffic control so that flights can be coordinated with other air traffic.
Record information in flight records, such as flight times, altitudes flown, and fuel consumption.
Supervise other crew members and direct activities during flights.
Perform minor aircraft maintenance and repair work, or arrange for major maintenance.
Check aircraft for defects and malfunctions, and monitor engine operation and aircraft systems during flights.
Install, repair, and replace equipment or accessories in aircraft.
Monitor the availability, use, or condition of lifesaving equipment or pollution preventatives.
Develop or present environmental compliance training or orientation sessions and advise on environmental policies.
Prepare hazardous waste manifests or notifications, and assist in budget implementation for compliance projects.
Assess the existing or potential environmental impact of projects, and inform company employees of environmental issues.
Examine equipment, processed materials, or reports to diagnose malfunctions and recommend design changes or upgrades.
Research catalogs or repair part lists to manage repair parts and requisition materials.
Read blueprints and specifications to determine layouts and manufacturing sequences for aircraft or machinery components.
Test or calibrate components or equipment, verifying performance and ensuring compliance with specifications.
Install plumbing or mechanical systems following manufacturer specifications and building codes.
Prepare, maintain, or revise documentation for quality assurance processes and environmental engineering projects.
Disassemble malfunctioning equipment and remove, repair, or replace defective parts using hand tools and power tools.
Install and repair plumbing or pipe systems, including fixtures and appliances, according to specifications.
Perform routine maintenance or repairs to restore systems to baseline operating conditions, including cleaning and lubricating components.
Design and plan layouts for equipment, including pipe systems and solar collector installations, based on specifications or blueprints.
Inspect, examine, or test installed systems or components using measuring instruments to ensure compliance with standards and proper functioning.
Fabricate parts needed for assembly or installation using shop machinery, tools, and equipment, ensuring accuracy according to specifications.
Calibrate equipment and verify accuracy of systems, including measurement and control systems for chemical processes.
Connect piping and seal joints using suitable equipment, including welding or soldering techniques.
Conduct research and develop processes for manufacturing or conducting experiments to improve and innovate systems or products.
Keep records of maintenance, repairs, and the status of systems while providing estimates of production costs and progress for management.
Train or supervise others in technical tasks, such as laying pipe installations, ensuring safety and operational procedures are followed.
Install and connect electrical assemblies, including monitoring systems and automated controls for pipe systems.
Perform adjustments and rework of machinery parts, ensuring alignment and fit for operational efficiency.
Test equipment or systems for leaks or performance issues by filling systems with water or air and observing pressure gauges.
Investigate structural integrity of operational sites and ensure compliance with safety regulations while operating or maintaining equipment.
Install or use precision instruments for measurement and leveling during assembly and construction processes.
Document inspection and test results and work performed or to be performed.
Obtain windshields or windows for specific automobile makes and models, and examine them for defects prior to installation.
Allow all glass parts installed with urethane ample time to cure, taking temperature and humidity into account.
Remove all dirt and foreign matter from damaged areas, apply primer along edges, and allow it to dry.
Maintain or repair vehicles with alternative fuel systems, such as biodiesel or hybrid vehicles.
Confer with customers, read work orders, or examine vehicles to determine the nature of damage.
Install replacement glass in vehicles and replace any moldings or clips that were removed.
Unbolt and remove wheels from vehicles using appropriate tools, and position safety jacks to support mine roofs until bolts can be installed.
Reassemble frames and reinstall engines after repairs, and adjust seats, doors, or windows.
Inspect machinery and vehicles to determine needed repairs, and monitor operations for adjustments.
Lubricate and clean equipment, perform maintenance and repair tasks, and ensure safe operations of machinery.
Analyze data to recommend placement of wells, assess costs, and verify drilling depths and alignments during operation.
Coordinate activities of workers engaged in research, planning, and development, and supervise assemblers and train employees.
Transfer electrical wiring and connect components, and keep records of drilling and production operations.
Evaluate findings to develop, design, or test equipment and processes in a mining environment.
Test and measure vehicle emissions to ensure compliance with environmental standards, and evaluate the effectiveness of operational improvements.
Implement green operational practices related to conservation of resources and efficiency in processes.
Inspect and test oil and gas wells, design equipment, and supervise the installation of safety measures and pollution controls.
Conduct engineering research experiments to modify machinery and improve operational efficiency in mining.
Prepare technical reports and summaries to communicate results to engineering and management staff.
Verify detonation of charges by observing control panels, or by listening for the sounds of blasts.
Light fuses and activate firing devices to set off single or multiple blasts.
Insert, pack, and pour explosives into blast holes and tamp material to compact charges.
Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites.
Maintain inventory levels of explosives and blasting supplies, ordering new supplies as necessary.
Repair and service blasting and automotive equipment, using hand tools.
Document geological formations encountered during work and mark patterns, locations, and depths of charge holes for drilling.
Place explosive charges in holes and detonate explosives to demolish structures or displace materials.
Place safety cones around blast areas and signal workers to clear blast sites prior to explosions.
Inspect blast areas to determine amounts and kinds of explosive charges needed and to ensure safety laws are observed.
Dismantle structures or equipment as needed.
Assemble, install, repair, and maintain elevators, escalators, and related equipment using tools and testing devices.
Test and repair circuits and sensors, following wiring and system specifications.
Read and interpret blueprints to determine the layout of system components and select installation equipment.
Inspect and test equipment to locate damage or worn parts and diagnose malfunctions.
Record maintenance and repair work performed on appliances or equipment.
Disassemble defective units for repair or replacement of parts using power tools and hand tools.
Lubricate moving parts and surfaces of mechanical parts.
Order replacement parts and maintain stocks of parts for repairs.
Connect electrical wiring to control panels and electric motors, and check that safety regulations are met.
Clean and reinstall parts, including internal compartments and moving parts, using cleaning compounds and rags.
Observe and test operation of appliances following installation, making any necessary initial adjustments.
Study blueprints and schematic diagrams to determine appropriate methods for installing or repairing systems.
Operate lifts, winches, or chain falls to move heavy objects into position.
Fabricate replacements for worn or broken parts using tools like welders, lathes, drill presses, and milling machines.
Install and secure structures such as door frames, railings, and other equipment using tools and power sources.
Test and repair track circuits and various signal systems to ensure compliance with safety standards.
Inspect job sites and equipment to ensure compliance with design specifications and safety standards.
Align, fit, and weld components according to specifications using tools such as jigs and welding torches.
Complete required documentation such as work orders, detailing services performed and materials used.
Use measuring tools to verify compliance of workpieces with specifications and address any discrepancies as needed.
Operate machines to move materials, such as coal or ore, into cars or onto conveyors, and manage transportation systems or components.
Design plans for transportation systems or parts, including airports, highways, and drainage structures, while ensuring environmental compliance.
Analyze proposals and designs related to water or wastewater treatment systems and recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques.
Develop engineering solutions for environmental risks and implement cleanup strategies for contaminated sites.
Design various water treatment plants and systems, such as pumping networks, distribution systems, and sludge treatment plants.
Conduct analyses to evaluate wastewater treatment methods, water quality, and determine methods for sludge disposal or treatment.
Perform feasibility studies, testing, and surveys to assess construction projects and their impacts on water resources.
Inspect and test equipment, circuits, and components to diagnose malfunctions or defects using diagnostic instruments.
Repair and maintain equipment and systems, including engines, pressure vessels, and other machinery, to ensure operational efficiency.
Train, supervise, or provide technical direction to junior technicians or other staff involved in engineering tasks.
Diagnose or repair problems with electronic or electrical equipment, replacing faulty components as needed.
Install equipment and accessories such as navigation, communication, and security systems.
Confer with customers to determine the nature of malfunctions and plan service requirements.
Set up and conduct tests of complete units and components under operational conditions.
Review project instructions and specifications to identify and modify requirements for fabrication and testing.
Analyze test results in relation to specifications and recommend modifications to equipment.
Prepare specifications, designs, or layouts for machines, components, or systems related to mechanical energy use.
Perform preventive or corrective maintenance on equipment and troubleshoot issues as needed.
Install, program, or repair robotic systems, including controllers and associated equipment.
Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or systems.
Select pattern materials such as wood, resin, and fiberglass, and apply coatings or paints to patterns used to produce plastic.
Develop, test, or program new robots, and train others to install, use, or maintain these robots.
Repair, rework, or calibrate hydraulic or pneumatic assemblies or systems to meet operational specifications or tolerances.
Operate metalworking machines to fabricate housings, jigs, fittings, or fixtures, and set up and operate other machine tools for production.
Verify part dimensions or clearances to ensure conformance to specifications using precision measuring instruments, and inspect parts for defects.
Align, fit, or assemble parts using hand or power tools, and assemble pattern sections using various fastening techniques.
Read blueprints, schematics, or technical orders to determine assembly methods and sequences, then mark and lay out reference points on materials.
Drill, countersink, and ream holes in parts for fasteners using power tools, and record specifications and production operations for standards.
Rework or alter components as required to meet standards, and align, fit, and join parts using bolts, screws, welding, or gluing.
Investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems, and provide technical guidance or support for robotic systems.
Consult and confer with engineering personnel to discuss developmental problems and recommend product modifications.
Create mechanical design documents for parts, assemblies, or finished products, and maintain formal engineering documents and databases.
Analyze system performance or operational requirements, and develop methodologies for transferring laboratory procedures to commercial production.
Coordinate or supervise the work of suppliers or vendors in designing, building, or testing nanosystem devices.
Prepare technical reports or presentations to communicate engineering results or recommendations to stakeholders.
Design advanced engineering systems for the automation of industrial tasks and develop control circuits for mechanical systems.
Connect power unit to machines or steam piping to equipment, and test unit to evaluate its mechanical operation.
Replace defective parts of machine, or adjust clearances and alignment of moving parts, and repair or replace damaged or worn parts.
Direct workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or cleaning parts, and assign schedules to work crews.
Fabricate needed parts or items from sheet metal, and cut and weld metal to repair broken metal parts, fabricate new parts, or assemble new equipment.
Document photonics system or component design processes, including objectives, issues, or outcomes, and write reports or proposals related to projects.
Build, configure, or test robots or robotic applications, and operate newly repaired machinery or equipment to verify the adequacy of repairs.
Test structures, controls, or mechanical, hydraulic, or electrical systems, according to test plans or in coordination with engineers, and analyze test results or machine error messages.
Adjust and maintain industrial machinery, using control and regulating devices, and perform routine maintenance on wind turbine equipment or other machinery.
Dismantle, clean, or reassemble machines and equipment for shipment to installation site, ensuring all repairs meet specifications.
Identify and recommend energy savings strategies, conducting energy audits to evaluate energy use and identify conservation measures.
Inspect or monitor energy systems to determine energy use or potential energy savings, and analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data.
Diagnose problems involving mechanical systems or electrical components to determine required repairs.
Identify materials appropriate for mechatronic system designs and create mechanical models to simulate mechatronic design concepts.
Determine the feasibility, costs, or performance benefits of new mechatronic equipment.
Observe and test the operation of machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions using testing devices, and examine parts for defects such as breakage or excessive wear.
Repair or maintain the operating condition of industrial production or processing machinery or equipment, and repair or replace malfunctioning components.
Disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts for repair and reassemble after completion of maintenance tasks.
Record parts or materials used, inventory and requisition supplies, and transport machine parts between work areas.
Set up and operate machines, adjust controls, and perform scheduled maintenance on units and components to ensure operational efficiency.
Conduct tests of machinery for operability before and after repairs, and inspect damaged machine parts to advise supervisors on repair needs.
Troubleshoot mechanical, hydraulic, or electrical malfunctions related to systems, and rewire or repair electrical systems as necessary.
Fabricate and dismantle parts, equipment, and machines using cutting equipment, and apply adhesives or lubrication according to specified procedures.
Select, install, and adjust machine tools or fixtures, and ensure proper functioning through testing and adjustments, including programming numerical control machines.
Collaborate with other workers to repair or move machinery and assess workplace environments through interviews or facility inspections.
Determine appropriate methods for fabricating and joining materials.
Monitor material performance and evaluate its deterioration.
Supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists.
Design and direct the testing or control of processing procedures.
Evaluate technical specifications and economic factors related to process or product design objectives.
Conduct or supervise tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure their quality.
Perform managerial functions, such as preparing proposals and budgets, analyzing labor costs, and writing reports.
Solve problems in various engineering fields including mechanical, chemical, electrical, civil, nuclear, and aerospace.
Plan and evaluate new projects, consulting with other engineers and corporate executives as necessary.
Review product plans and recommend material selection based on design objectives like strength, weight, and cost.
Design processing plants and equipment and modify properties of metal alloys using thermal and mechanical treatments.
Guide technical staff in developing materials for specific uses in projected products or devices.
Plan and implement laboratory operations to develop material and fabrication procedures that meet standards.
Conduct training sessions on new material products, applications, or manufacturing methods for customers.
Write programs for CNC machines to cut metal and plastic materials.
Write articles for technical magazines, journals, and trade association publications.
Conduct tests on materials and components to replicate their characteristics using computers.
Set machine stops or guides to specified lengths as indicated by templates or rules.
Inspect workpieces for defects and measure them to determine accuracy of machine operation.
Monitor feed and speed of machines during the machining process and compute data applying shop mathematics.
Operate machines, such as lathes and grinders, and perform minor maintenance and repairs as necessary.
Evaluate product failure data and laboratory test results, and investigate equipment failures to determine solutions.
Prepare technical reports, sketches, working drawings, specifications, proposals, and budgets for engineering projects.
Design and oversee testing, installation, and repair of marine apparatus and equipment, and conduct performance tests.
Discuss plans with clients, contractors, and other engineers to evaluate and implement necessary changes.
Install doors, hatches, brackets, and clips.
Prepare or present technical or project status reports.
Establish production or quality control standards.
Read current literature or attend meetings to stay abreast of new automotive technology or competitive products.
Design or engineer filtration systems to reduce harmful emissions and analyze biofuel performance specifications for aerospace applications.
Design fuel cell systems, including subsystems, stacks, and components, and conduct analyses to characterize their performance.
Implement fuel cell testing or development programs and directly supervise automotive testing.
Conduct post-service or failure analyses using electromechanical diagnostic principles.
Develop materials or test equipment for fuel cells and manage hybrid system architecture.
Fabricate prototypes of fuel cell components and coordinate engineering schedules with manufacturing departments.
Fit and align fabricated parts for welding or assembly and lay out metal structural parts from blueprints.
Design control systems for automotive applications, including energy and emissions management.
Conduct automotive design reviews and develop engineering specifications for design concepts.
Plan and develop layouts for fabrication processes, including cutting, welding, and machining operations.
Write, review, or maintain engineering documentation and prepare test stations for component testing.
Perform failure or root cause analyses and provide technical direction to engineers or support personnel.
Measure workpieces and verify conformance to specifications using various measuring instruments.
Compile production output records and compute layout dimensions based on specifications.
Start machines, monitor operations, and make necessary adjustments for optimal performance.
Preheat workpieces using heating furnaces or hand torches.
Thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders; lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.
Compute machine indexings and settings for specified dimensions and base reference points; calculate machine speed and feed ratios and the size and position of cuts.
Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces or control coolant systems to ensure proper lubrication and cooling during operation.
Move machine controls to index workpieces, activate machine start-up switches, and set controls to regulate operation according to specifications.
Study blueprints, work orders, or machining instructions to determine product specifications, tool requirements, and operational sequences.
Select machine tooling and set cutting speeds, feed rates, and depths of cuts according to machining instructions or knowledge of material properties.
Measure workpieces and lay out work using precision measuring devices; examine and measure finished workpieces to verify conformance to specifications.
Set and adjust machine controls according to product specifications; observe machine operations to detect and correct any problems.
Activate shears and grinders to trim workpieces and use grinding tools to finish surfaces and remove burrs or sharp edges.
Inspect or measure raw materials and finished products to ensure they meet specifications, and troubleshoot equipment as needed.
Maintain machines and perform preventative maintenance, including cleaning machines and replacing broken or worn machine tools using hand tools.
Direct and train other workers in equipment operation and material handling processes; signal and assist workers to remove and position equipment and feed machines.
Control coolant systems and maintain proper levels of fluids, such as oil and coolant, in machines.
Innovate or improve tooling or machine operations based on analysis of job orders, specifications, and observed machine performance.
Adjust or maintain equipment, including lasers, microscopes, pulse generators, and optical devices.
Assemble and build optical components and prototype optomechanical devices for equipment such as cameras or telescopes.
Repair or calibrate equipment, such as surgical lasers, and test fiber cables with mechanical connectors.
Set up or operate assembly or processing equipment and prototype or test apparatus, including lasers and recording equipment.
Visually examine materials and structures for corrosion or flaws using tools like endoscopes and ultrasonic devices.
Develop or utilize new non-destructive testing methods such as acoustic testing and leak testing to evaluate materials.
Document non-destructive testing processes and results and conduct examinations via radiographic techniques.
Inspect completed work for conformance to standards using tools and document operational records and statistics.
Supervise non-destructive testing personnel and assist engineers in developing new products, tools, or processes.
Requisition materials and maintain records of time, materials, expenditures, and employee training.
Monitor tool inventories, examine work areas, and enforce safety regulations to ensure compliance and prevent unsafe conditions.
Resolve employee problems, confer with personnel to coordinate activities, and conduct training in safety and equipment use.
Prepare reports on work activities, conduct evaluations of worker performance, and participate in budget preparation.
Inspect and test materials or equipment for safety, and manage emergency response for incidents and accidents.
Plan work assignments and determine equipment allocations based on priorities and personnel skills.
Counsel employees about work-related issues and recommend personnel actions for hiring or evaluation.
Prepare and maintain operational standards while monitoring adherence to safety rules during work activities.
Confer with customers and personnel to exchange information, resolve problems, and improve services.
